description: Arabic-Hebrew cognate identification (2-class: TRUE_COGNATE vs FALSE_FRIEND only, no LOANWORD) at word level

definitions.TRUE_COGNATE: Words that share a common Semitic root AND have semantically overlapping meanings. At least one core meaning must be present in both languages.
definitions.FALSE_FRIEND: Words that have similar orthographic or phonological forms BUT have completely different meanings, with no meaningful semantic overlap.

undiac.name: Cognate identification / 2-class / word level / undiac
undiac.template:
You are given an Arabic word and a Hebrew word.

Your task is to determine whether they are:
- TRUE_COGNATE
- FALSE_FRIEND

Answer with only one label: TRUE_COGNATE or FALSE_FRIEND.

Definitions:

TRUE_COGNATE:
Words that share a common Semitic root AND have semantically overlapping meanings.
At least one core meaning must be present in both languages.

FALSE_FRIEND:
Words that have similar orthographic or phonological forms BUT have completely different meanings, with no meaningful semantic overlap.

Arabic: {arabic_word}
Hebrew: {hebrew_word}

Answer:

diac.name: Cognate identification / 2-class / word level / diac
diac.template:
You are given a fully diacritized Arabic word and a fully diacritized Hebrew word.

Your task is to determine whether they are:
- TRUE_COGNATE
- FALSE_FRIEND

Answer with only one label: TRUE_COGNATE or FALSE_FRIEND.

Definitions:

TRUE_COGNATE:
Words that share a common Semitic root AND have semantically overlapping meanings.
At least one core meaning must be present in both languages.

FALSE_FRIEND:
Words that have similar diacritized forms (matching consonantal skeletons AND vowel patterns) BUT have completely different meanings, with no meaningful semantic overlap.

Arabic: {arabic_word_diac}
Hebrew: {hebrew_word_diac}

Answer:

uroman.name: Cognate identification / 2-class / word level / uroman
uroman.template:
You are given an Arabic word and a Hebrew word in transliteration by uroman.

Your task is to determine whether they are:
- TRUE_COGNATE
- FALSE_FRIEND

Answer with only one label: TRUE_COGNATE or FALSE_FRIEND.

Definitions:

TRUE_COGNATE:
Words that share a common Semitic root AND have semantically overlapping meanings.
At least one core meaning must be present in both languages.

FALSE_FRIEND:
Words that have similar romanized forms (reflecting similar phonological or orthographic structure) BUT have completely different meanings, with no meaningful semantic overlap.

Arabic: {arabic_word_uroman}
Hebrew: {hebrew_word_uroman}

Answer:

ipa.name: Cognate identification / 2-class / word level / ipa
ipa.template:
You are given an Arabic word and a Hebrew word in IPA transcription.

Your task is to determine whether they are:
- TRUE_COGNATE
- FALSE_FRIEND

Answer with only one label: TRUE_COGNATE or FALSE_FRIEND.

Definitions:

TRUE_COGNATE:
Words that share a common Semitic root AND have semantically overlapping meanings.
At least one core meaning must be present in both languages.

FALSE_FRIEND:
Words that have similar phonetic forms in IPA transcription (reflecting similar pronunciation) BUT have completely different meanings, with no meaningful semantic overlap.

Arabic: {arabic_word_ipa}
Hebrew: {hebrew_word_ipa}

Answer:
